key_insight: The paper argues that spectral bias in neural models can be overcome by using a fractal, prime-harmonic encoding that better preserves the fine-scale structure of chaotic attractors.
engineering_relevance: If effective, this approach could substantially extend long-horizon forecasting for chaotic dynamical systems beyond what standard Transformers and Neural Operators can capture.
method: Uses a Fractal Neural Operator (FNO) architecture for continuous dynamical systems.
Introduces a Harmonic Weierstrass Encoder based on non-resonant prime-number harmonics.
Claims fractal latent embeddings avoid the spectral gaps seen in geometric encodings like $2^k$.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that FNO predicts the Lorenz-63 system for 347 Lyapunov times and outperforms Reservoir Computing baselines by 2.3×.
watch_item: The method may be sensitive to encoding design and numerical stability, so the claimed gains need careful independent validation on additional chaotic systems.
paper_title: The Fractal Neural Operator: Overcoming Spectral Bias in Chaotic Attractors via Prime-Harmonic Weierstrass Encodings
